issue_title: Listing federated LDAP users is very slow with import enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

When users are imported from LDAP, subsequent requests to fetch the users (for example when going to the Users  page in the Admin Console) take a much longer time to return.

If import is disabled and users are fetched all the time from LDAP, the response time to fetch users in the Users screen is barely affected and the screen loads almost instantly. However, once we enable the switch to import users, the first request fetches users (and import them!) very fast, but once they are stored in the local DB all subsequent requests take a much longer time to complete - around 12 seconds in my setup when fetching only the first 10 users, which are now all stored in the local DB.

This has probably something to do with the `validate` method that runs as part of the search and is responsible for creating the proxied user with all the mappers. Each user has to be searched on LDAP to check for its existence and fetch updated data, so when fetching 10 federated users from the local DB we have 10 corresponding LDAP queries. Debugging the `validate` method, it looks like this search can be performed multiple times during a single request, which must be impacting the performance of bulding the proxy objects significantly.

We need to investigate/profile the server in order to understand why the performance with the imported users is so much worse than when fetching users all the time from LDAP (i.e. import disabled).

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The performance to fetch 10 federated users that were imported into the Keycloak DB should not (much) worse than fetching these users directly from LDAP all the time.

### Actual behavior

Performance to fetch users drop significantly once they are imported into the Keycloak DB.

### How to Reproduce?

- Setup a LDAP federation with at least 1000 users.
- Import the users from LDAP
- Go to the Admin Console, click on the Users link
- Check the time it takes to fetch the federated users from the local DB

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure searched LDAPObject is properly cached before other methods that trigger user validation run

Closes #34050

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

### Area

### Describe the bug

When using the Evaluate subtab of Client Scopes, in a client's settings, in the admin UI, and clicking the Users input, the focus goes to the dropdown instead of the search bar. This means it needs a second click to be able to start typing a username.
While this may be desirable for very small deployments, as soon as you have >20 users, it's not great.

### Version

nightly for this test, 25.0.6 in prod, and 23.0.5 in other instances

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When I click onto the Users field, I can start typing a username.

### Actual behavior

The focus goes to the dropdown list of users. If I have more users than fit in one page of results, I need to click a second time in the field to be able to start typing.

### How to Reproduce?

- Spin up a blank Keycloak instance
- Go to the admin UI, Clients, `account`, Client Scopes, Evaluate
- Try typing something like your admin user's username
- See that it doesn't type, and you need to click it again

issue_title: Users without `view-realm` can't see user lockout state in Admin UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

If a user has `view-users`, `query-users`, `query-groups` and `manage-users` roles but **not** `view-realm` (in `realm-management`), they are unable to see user lockout state in Admin UI.

This set of `realm-management` permissions is common for a user in a "customer support" role. One of the use cases for this persona is the ability to see and toggle user lockout state.

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The user should be able to see:

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a50a8035-b719-4f8f-8b17-14bf5a00371d)
-----
and toggle lockout state:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dd953505-5243-44d7-86f8-972ee10ec28f)


### Actual behavior

The lockout state and toggle is hidden from the user.

### How to Reproduce?

1. create a realm
2. create a user1 in that realm with `view-users`, `query-users`, `query-groups` and `manage-users` roles 
3. create a separate user2 with no other roles
4. configure brute force detection for a small number of failed logins to produce a temporary lockout
5. attempt to login as user2 to the account console with a bad password > than the failed login threshold
6. log into the new realm's security admin console using the user1 you created
7. find the user in the Users tab. see that you can't see the lockout state in the list.
8. click on the user. see that you can't see the lockout toggle in the form


### Anything else?

Relevant code is in the `EditUser.tsx` form, where it looks in the realm to see if it has the `bruteForceProtected` flag enabled:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.0.0/js/apps/admin-ui/src/user/EditUser.tsx#L156

Which it doesn't when the user lacks `view-realm`, because the `RealmAdminResource` only allows a few realm attributes:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.0.0/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/RealmAdminResource.java#L381-L390
commit_message:
add brute force enabled so we can render switch (#34282)

fixes: #34065

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>

issue_title: Keycloak 26.0.0/26.0.1 Import Issue: Multiple Realms Not Imported, Duplicated Realm Imported Instead
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

I am encountering an issue with Keycloak versions 26.0.0 and 26.0.1 when attempting to import multiple realms through the file-based import method. Specifically, I am mounting a directory with multiple realm configuration files at /opt/keycloak/data/import/. However, instead of all realms being imported, only one realm is imported repeatedly, overwriting the others.

### Version

26.0.1, 26.0.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

All provided realms should be imported.

### Actual behavior

Only one realm is imported multiple times, overwriting any other realms. There seems to be no pattern based on the alphabetical order or file structure of the realms in the directory.



### How to Reproduce?

1. Create multiple realm files (e.g., realm1.json, realm2.json, realm3.json).
2. Mount these realm files to /opt/keycloak/data/import/.
3. Start Keycloak and check the imported realms.


### Anything else?

In the logs, I repeatedly see messages like:
``` 
[org.keycloak.exportimport.util.ImportUtils] (main) Realm 'realm2' already exists. Removing it before import 
[org.keycloak.exportimport.util.ImportUtils] (main) Realm 'realm2' imported
```

Additional Details:
When attempting to create a new realm through the Admin GUI after encountering this issue, I receive the following error:
```
409 Conflict: "Client id 'account-console' already exists"
```

fix: providing a separate session for each file (#34210)

issue_title: JS password validation doesn't work as intended with uppercase and lowercase minimum requirements
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

When having a minimum number of uppercase and lowercase characters required for new passwords, they're not correctly validated during registration. 

The code doesn't check if there are enough characters of the required type, but if the amount of characters of all other types is bigger than the set minimum.

Uppercase:
`value.split("").filter((char) => char !== char.toUpperCase()).length > policy.value`

Lowercase:
`value.split("").filter((char) => char !== char.toLowerCase()).length > policy.value`


### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No error messages are shown if the password contains the minimum amount of uppercase and lowercase characters.

### Actual behavior

Error messages are shown despite the password containing the minimum amount of uppercase and lowercase characters.

### How to Reproduce?

- Use any realm with registration enabled
- Go to Authentication -> Policies -> Password policy and enable uppercase chars & lowercase chars and click on Save
- Go to the registration page of the realm
- Type `TEst` in the password field and leave it to let the JavaScript code validate it with the policies
  - It results in error messages
- Type `1234` in the password field and leave it to let the JavaScript code validate it with the policies
  - It results in no errors at all

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix password validation with uppercase and lowercase minimum requirements (#34152)

Closes #34151

issue_title: OIDC IdP Unable to validate signatures using validatingPublicKey certificate
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Pasting the public key to the validatingPublicKey field in PEM format is not working as before.
The UI field replaces the newlines with spaces and the Keycloak backend wrongly strips the PEM headers and footers with a greedy regex replaceAll in PemUtils, resulting in nullifying the public key, before validating the external token.

In my opinion this bug is caused by both a recent UI change and the wrong regular expression in PemUtilsProvider.java line 139.

The external token validation using a PEM formatted public key, was working in Keycloak 23.

### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Pasting the PEM formatted key in the validatingPublicKey field used a text box form type before.

### Actual behavior

In KC25 the validatingPublicKey field is now a one line text field and the public key is corrupted.

### How to Reproduce?

Paste a PEM formatted public key to a OIDC idp validatingPublicKey field.
Validate an external token signed with the matching private key using a tokenExchange strategy.

### Anything else?

A workaround for bypassing the greedy replaceAll is to remove the PEM headers,
-----BEGIN* and -----END*
commit_message:
changed to use TextArea (#34539)

fixes: #34201

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>

issue_title: logout with client_id and/or post_logout_redirect_uri results in bad request on logout confirmation page
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

When using the url to logout with a client id and post_logout_redirect_uri, after the user clicked on the logout button, they often get shown a bad request error:
```
https://<keycloak-host>/auth/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/logout?client_id=the-client&post_logout_redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fexample.com/
```
This also happens when only providing the client_id.

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

- After clicking on "logout" on the logout page, the user should be redirected to the url given in `post_logout_redirect_uri`

### Actual behavior

Sometimes, the user receives a Bad request response and the log shows an invalid token error.

### How to Reproduce?

With those steps, I could reproduce it easily. But it seems not only the logout url is affected, but others as well, as the normal userflow on our page does not contain the logout flow without parameters:

* login via login page
* enter the logout page without any parameters: `/auth/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/logout`
* enter the logout page with client_id and redirect: `/auth/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/logout?client_id=the-client&post_logout_redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fexample.com/`
* click on Logout
* A bad request is shown

### Anything else?

This seems to happen when the user has not been on the keycloak page for some time. But after a fresh login it would work.
When the user refreshes the logout page before clicking the logout button, it will also work correctly.

The refresh seems to reset the `KC_RESTART` cookie, which will then be valid.
The error in keycloak log states an invalid token.

In my steps to reproduce, there is no "Back to Application" link shown. But in the normal userflow there always is, but I could not find a reason why the token seemed to be invalidated. Maybe some token refresh in the background?
Our auth proxy creates a new access token from the refresh token every minute, so maybe it has something to do with that?

issue_title: Deleting a user leads to ISPN marshalling exception
issue_body:
## Version

issue_title: Group search in user view doesn't work as expected for nested groups
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I have encountered this bug already in the version 23.0.6.: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/28079 It still occurrs in 26.0.1.

Group search in user view doesn't work as expected for nested groups. Top-level groups including matching subgroup are displayed instead of the actual hit.

Let's assume that my hierarchy looks like this:

Top_Group_1 > Top_Group_2 > Matching_Subgroup

Then result will look like this:

Top_Group_1/Top_Group_2

Even when I navigate to 'Top_Group_2' and search for the matching subgroup there, 'Top_Group_1/Top_Group_2' will be displayed as a hit. It feels counterintuitive.

### Version

26.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Search returns subgroup matching provided search term, not the top level group.

### Actual behavior

Search returns top level group of the matching subgroup, not the subgroup itself.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Go to Users
2. Select a user
3. Go to Groups Tab
4. Click 'Join Group'
5. Type search term matching one of the nested groups
6. Top level group instead of a subgroup is returned

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Service accounts visible under user search in Admin console
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug In Keycloak version 26.0.1, the introduction of the `exact` parameter in the `/admin/realms/master/ui-ext/brute-force-user` endpoint has resulted in unintended filtering behavior for service accounts. When the `exact` parameter is not specified, it defaults to false, which inadvertently affects the search results for users, particularly service accounts. ### Version 26.0.1 ### Regression - [X] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Service accounts should not be visible under user search in Admin console. ### Actual behavior Service accounts visible under user search in Admin console. ### How to Reproduce? Just create client with service accounts enabled and see the users!. ### Anything else? Below is the change in admin ui that caused the issue . I feel instead of making change in frontend , we can improve backend see the below snip. `/admin-ui-ext/src/main/java/org/keycloak/admin/ui/rest/BruteForceUsersResource.java` Because of `exact` flag in request the `else if`(Line:133) part is getting executed and the we are setting service-account flag by default to true! Can we change that to false ?

issue_title: PEM files distributed as part of SAML adapter configs are missing -----BEGIN and -----END blocks
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

When you use the SAML apapter config downloads to download a client config, you are given a certificate and private key that are "bare", just a base64 blob starting with MII...but most clients that recognize pem files (including mod_auth_mellon) do not recognize these without their "container" lines.

### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

pem files that are valid according to https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7468

### Actual behavior

Bare pem files such as the following, with no -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE----/-----END CERTIFICATE  and -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY----/-----END PRIVATE KEY----- blocks.  Tools such as mod_auth_mellon seem to reject these.  Keycloak's own docs for mod_auth_mellon seem to say that these are usable when downloaded from keycloak.

```
MIICuTCCAaECBgGSu5MBETANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQsFADAgMR4wHAYDVQQDDBVodHRwczovL2Nsb2FrLmlzYy5vcmcwHhcNMjQxMDIzMjI0ODQ2WhcNMzQxMDIzMjI1MDI2WjAgMR4wHAYDVQQDDBVodHRwczovL2Nsb2FrLmlzYy5vcmcwggEiM....etc....contents not important just missing headers
```

### How to Reproduce?

Follow directions here: https://www.keycloak.org/securing-apps/mod-auth-mellon to download the adapter configs, and try to use them.

### Anything else?

On FreeBSD 14.1 AMD64 with apache24-2.4.62 and with ap24-mod_auth_mellon-0.19.1.
commit_message:
fix: PEM files distributed as part of SAML adapter configs for mod_auth_mellon export

Changing return type of ClientResource from String to Response to support different response types. Should not be breaking as this is just a class used internally by Keycloak integration tests.

Closes #34276

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When using a flow with 
- `[Required] Username Form` 
- `[Required] Password Form` 
- `[Conditional] Subflow`
  -  `[Required] Condition - user configured` 
  - `[Required] Conditional OTP Form`

... you get an NPE when the user has configured OTP:

```
WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-38) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.models.AuthenticatorConfigModel.getConfig()" because the return value of "org.keycloak.authentication.AuthenticationFlowContext.getAuthenticatorConfig()" is null
```

The error does not ooccur if using `Username Password Form` instead, but the example above is a simplified version of a more complex flow. 

A partial realm export with an authentication flow where with the problem is appended.

[test-conditional-otp-realm-export.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17503765/test-conditional-otp-realm-export.json)


### Version

26.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

To be able to log in by entering username in first form, then password in the second form, then finally, if configured, OTP in the third form.

### Actual behavior

- Entering username in first form successfully.
- Entering password in second form succefully.
- If OTP has been configured for the account the authentication flow ends with error message `We are sorry... Unexpected error when handling authentication request to identity provider.`

issue_title: Fix DB overflow for EVENT_ENTITY table and SESSION_ID column in case that incorrect data are sent
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I noticed that I'm getting Stacktraces when a client (Rancher) tries to receive a token. I suspected to be inserted in the column so I temporarily altered the table `EVENT_ID`.`SESSION_ID` to be of type text instead and I noticed that the full encoded token is inserted in the session id:

```
Oct 24 15:23:42 X000VV30 kc.sh[1734733]: 2024-10-24 15:23:42,192 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-14) type="CODE_TO_TOKEN_ERROR", realmId="107f816d-85b9-4408-90d5-4e17371f6bd9", realmName="sso", clientId="rancher", userId="null", sessionId="", ipAddress="10.45.194.242", error="invalid_code", grant_type="authorization_code", code_id="", client_auth_method="client-secret"

[root@X000VV30 root]# mysql -D keycloak
Reading table information for completion of table and column names
You can turn off this feature to get a quicker startup with -A

Welcome to the MariaDB monitor.  Commands end with ; or \g.
Your MariaDB connection id is 518
Server version: 10.5.22-MariaDB MariaDB Server

Copyright (c) 2000, 2018, Oracle, MariaDB Corporation Ab and others.

Type 'help;' or '\h' for help. Type '\c' to clear the current input statement.

MariaDB [keycloak]> SELECT SESSION_ID FROM EVENT_ENTITY LIMIT 1;
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SESSION_ID                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.001 sec)

```

### Version

26.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The authentication to fail with a proper or an empty SESSION_ID instead of the token/code being inserted in the EVENT_ENTITY table

### Actual behavior

The encoded token is inserted and causes a Stacktrace to be printed for every attempt.

### How to Reproduce?

I'm not sure what Rancher(v2.9.1) does to provoke this error, we followed their steps [here](https://ranchermanager.docs.rancher.com/how-to-guides/new-user-guides/authentication-permissions-and-global-configuration/authentication-config/configure-keycloak-oidc) for the integration and followed every step

### Anything else?

Logs from the service: 
```

issue_title: ParEndpoint#request corrupts values added in request object
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When sending a request object with an array with JSON objects, e.g. as RAR (Rich Authorization Request) input for `authorization_details`, via PAR (Pushed Authorization Request), the logic in [org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.par.endpoints.ParEndpoint#request()](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/par/endpoints/ParEndpoint.java#L155) corrupts the sent value, because the '[' or ']' characters are effectivly removed from the string.

Related to this issue, the [RAR spec says that authorization_details parameter](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9396#section-2) in the request object must be a JSON array.

Example:

```
POST https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/ext/par/request
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

response_type=code&client_id=dummy-client&client_secret=dummy-secret&nonce=abc123456&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fapps.acme.test%3A4633%2Fwebapp%2Flogin%2Foauth2%2Fcode%2Fkeycloak&scope=openid%20profile&authorization_details=%5B%7B%22type%22%3A%20%22urn%3Aopenfinanceuae%3Aaccount-access-consent%3Av1.0%22%2C%22foo%22%3A%22bar%22%7D%5D
```


Raw authorization_details:
```
[{"type": "urn:openfinanceuae:account-access-consent:v1.0","foo":"bar"},{"type": "urn:openfinanceuae:account-access-consent:v1.0","gugu":"gaga"}]
```

authorization_details encoded via:
```
encodeURIComponent('[{"type": "urn:openfinanceuae:account-access-consent:v1.0","foo":"bar"},{"type": "urn:openfinanceuae:account-access-consent:v1.0","gugu":"gaga"}]')
```
Yields:
```
'%5B%7B%22type%22%3A%20%22urn%3Aopenfinanceuae%3Aaccount-access-consent%3Av1.0%22%2C%22foo%22%3A%22bar%22%7D%2C%7B%22type%22%3A%20%22urn%3Aopenfinanceuae%3Aaccount-access-consent%3Av1.0%22%2C%22gugu%22%3A%22gaga%22%7D%5D'
```

After processing via `org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.par.endpoints.ParEndpoint#request()` the decoded value stored in the `SingleUseObjectProvider` is  - note the missing brackets.
```
{"type": "urn:openfinanceuae:account-access-consent:v1.0","foo":"bar"},{"type": "urn:openfinanceuae:account-access-consent:v1.0","gugu":"gaga"}
```

cc @tnorimat @dcreado


### Version

26.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The ParEndpoint should not transform the received value. If a transformation is required it should be applied by a suitable protocol mapper.

### Actual behavior

ParEndpoint removes the '[' or ']' characters from additional (not known) parameters.

### How to Reproduce?

See above.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Revise PAR request object parameter handlig (#34352)

We now store the original parameter value as-is, in case only a single parameter value is provided. In case multiple parameter values are provided
for the same parameter, we only retain the first parameter.
This ensures that the original value is retained. Previously the value list from the
`decodedFormParameters` `MultivaluedMap` was converted to a String while replacing '[' and ']'
with an empty string, which corrupted the original parameter values stored.

Fixes #34352

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Admin UI doesn't show realms when using login through identity provider
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug If we do login through username+password admin UI works wonderfully... but if we add an identity provider there it doesn't work... The ext-ui resources, specifically `UIRealmsResource` are now called inside every/each realm... when logged into console admin, initially the admin UI works, but when we change to a different realm other than master, the realm list fails. And it is because the Identity Provider only exists in the master realm, as we don't need it in the other realms, since this is an identity provider just to manage logins to Kecloak admin console, and manage all realms. The Request Path after switching from master realm to a Test realm in the admin console is: 1 - Admin UI requests`http://keycloak.localhost/auth/admin/realms/Test/ui-ext/realms/names?first=0&max=11&search=` to list the realms 2 - `AdminPermissions.realms(session, auth.adminAuth());` is invoked 3 - ` UserSessionUtil.findValidSession(session, adminsRealm, accessToken, event, client)` is invoked 4 - `AuthenticationManager.isSessionValid(realm, userSession)` is invoked 5 - `if (userSession.getNote(Details.IDENTITY_PROVIDER) != null) {` will be true as this is a login done through an identity provider 6 - `if (realm.getIdentityProviderByAlias(brokerAlias) == null) {` condition will also be true as the identity provider only exists in master realm and now the request is done in the context of each realm in Keycloak (Test realm in this example) and so will return false 7 - Because ^ returned false (AuthenticationManager line 186), it will `throw error.invalidToken("Session expired")` even though the session is not expired... If I do login with the same user but using username + password and not using the identity provider, everything works... So there is a bug in Admin UI when we do login to keycloak administration console using an identity provider. ### Version 26.0.2 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Admin UI works with Identity Provider login ### Actual behavior Admin UI doesn't work with Identity Provider login ### How to Reproduce? Just run Keycloak locally with a docker container. Create a trial account in Okta or if you have another Identity Provider (you can use another Keycloak container and use that instead) Configure an identity provider. Link an existing admin account to the identity provider. Login to Keycloak Admin UI using identity provider instead of username + password. And then using the realm drop down switch to a different realm than master... In the different relam now, try to open the realm drop down, it will just have an spinner, and taking a look at the requests you will see that you've got a 401. ![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a43a5419-8b77-4f74-a424-8804c521c016) ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: User import gets exponentially slow
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

I have an Keycloak instance with 10k users. When doing an upgrade to a new version, we export all users in multiple files with 50 users per file (default from Keycloak) and then import the new realm config and all those user files.

During import I noticed that the time it takes to import a single users file is about 1 second on the first file and with each new files it increases exponentially up to 20 seconds per file.

While researching this bug i found the issue #20703. 
It was resolved by adding this note to the [Importing and Exporting Realms](https://www.keycloak.org/server/importExport) guide:
> Exporting and importing into single files can produce large files, so if your database contains more than 500 users, export to a directory and not a single file. 
> Using a directory performs better as the directory provider **uses a separate transaction** for each "page" (a file of users).

Apparently this doesn't apply to the import. My guess is: All user files are being imported in **a single transaction**, which get's bigger and bigger with each batch of 50 users, resulting in exponentially longer import times with each file.

### Version

26.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The time it takes to import a single users file stays the same.

### Actual behavior

The time it takes to import a single users file increases exponentially with each file imported.

### How to Reproduce?

- Get yourself an Keycloak instance with a few thousand users
- (CLI) Export the realm with these flags: --users different_files --users-per-file 50
- (CLI) Import the realm and all user files from the directory to which they were exported
- Notice the time it takes for each file to be import increasing with each import

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Incorrect Content-Type Expectation for POST /admin/realms/{realm}/organizations/{id}/members in Keycloak API
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When sending a request to the POST /admin/realms/{realm}/organizations/{id}/members endpoint, Keycloak expects the content type to be application/json. However, it only accepts a plain text string (e.g., `95f019a8-977f-b7d5-f781-c1b2a3ef288c`) rather than JSON-encoded data (`"95f019a8-977f-b7d5-f781-c1b2a3ef288c"`). This lead to the following issue with an auto-generated rest api client: https://github.com/fschick/Keycloak.RestApiClient/issues/11 where to client would convert the input into a json string with double quotes but the Keycloak api expects a plain string without double quotes.

### Version

26.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Either the api should change the [definition](https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/latest/rest-api/openapi.json) to match the expected content type from application/json to text/plain or it accepts a JSON-encoded string.

### Actual behavior

Currently the definition is set to application/json.

### How to Reproduce?

I think https://github.com/fschick/Keycloak.RestApiClient/issues/11 shows pretty accurately what the issue is.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: LDAP: searching users with import disabled is slower since fix for 34050
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

With the fix for https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/34050 the search for LDAP users with import disabled became much slower.

When import is disabled, the in-memory user proxy is created and cached in the transaction, and subsequent validations didn't have to search LDAP for the user because the corresponding LDAPObject is already cached.

With the changes to fix the slowness with import enabled, the `loadAndValidateUser` method is now calling LDAP again for each user, ignoring that the LDAPObject is already available via the cached proxied user

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Search for the LDAP users with import disabled should be very fast (< 500ms)

### Actual behavior

Search for LDAP users is now taking around the same time for both imported and not imported scenarios, as now every user is searched in LDAP again in both cases

### How to Reproduce?

Start keycloak main with a LDAP provider containing around 2k LDAP users, import enabled = false. Go to Users page and check the time it takes to load the first 10 users (around 5.5s)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Do not rely on the `pwdLastSet` attribute when updating AD entries
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

We only update the status of the user, if enabled or disabled, if the `pwdLastSet` attribute is set to its corresponding LDAP entry in AD.

As a consequence, we do not show a proper error message when trying to update the user to indicate that the account cannot be updated due to the password not yet set. 

I'm not sure why we have this constraint so the proposal is to ignore the `pwdLastSet` attribute when updating the user and create the user as enabled just like when using vendors other than AD.

From a security perspective, the account is effectively not able to authenticate because no credential is set and AD user controls like `password_expired` will still work to force a password reset.

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/31456#issuecomment-2269846405.

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When creating the user in AD, the user should be created just line any other user where the account is enabled by default.

### Actual behavior

When creating the user in AD, the user is disabled and you are forced to first set a password and then enable the user.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create an user in AD. The user is created as disabled.
* Try to enable the user

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not rely on the pwdLastSet attribute when updating AD entries

Closes #34467

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Keycloak OpenAPI specification doesn't match actual API implementation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When sending a POST request to create a group to the Admin API (/admin/realms/{realm}/groups) the documentation and OpenAPI specification suggest that I can expect a 200 OK response. In reality a 201 Created response gets returned.
This is especially troublesome when using auto-generated clients using NSwag, etc.

### Version

26.0.4

- [ ] The issue is a regression

OpenAPI specification should match the actual API behavior.

OpenAPI specification does not match actual API behavior.

- Expect 200 Response
- Get 201 Response

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug Clicking on the (?) button in the Keycloak admin UI opens up a pop-up menu with a button labeled "Documentation". Clicking this link in Keycloak versions prior to v25 will bring you to https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html. Clicking this link in version 25+, including the latest v26.0.4 does nothing - does not open the link in a new tab or otherwise. ![keycloak_26_link_broken](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af0c5a44-abd3-4165-870b-16071d8801a6) Tested using the Keycloak docker container: * `docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME=admin -e KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26.0.4 start-dev` - clicking the link does nothing. * `docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:25.0 start-dev` - clicking the link does nothing * `docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:24.0 start-dev` - clicking the link opens the Keycloak documentation in a new tab. Browsers I've seen the issue on: * Firefox 131.0.3 (Windows), * Edge 130.0.2849.56 (Windows), * Firefox 128.3.0esr (Rocky Linux). ### Version 26.0.4 ### Regression - [X] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Clicking on the Documentation link in the admin UI should open https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html in the user's browser. ### Actual behavior Clicking on the Documentation link does nothing, no errors logged in the console, no network requests made. ### How to Reproduce? issue_title: Renaming realm in UI broken
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In a freshly install using keycloak operator(26.0.4) in kubernetes, it is not possible to rename the "master" realm through the Admin UI.
PUT request to the endpoint gets response:
 `errorMessage	"Can't rename master realm"`

If I create a new realm and try to rename the new one the same UI behaviour will happen.
Now the put request states:
`error	"HTTP 403 Forbidden"`

### Version

26.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Realm would rename and no error should appear in the UI or an error message showing the real error from PUT request "errorMessage	"Can't rename master realm""

### Actual behavior

An error screen stating "Realm not found" and realm is not renamed.
Browser URL changes to point to renamed realm url.
Realm drop down stops working
![Screenshot_20241031_110728](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16f65a71-070c-4ff4-b8ef-e77b3b571fe7)


### How to Reproduce?

Using a fresh local postgresdb.
Keycloak manifest used in setup.
```
apiVersion: k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1
kind: Keycloak
metadata:
  name: keycloak
spec:
  proxy:
    headers: "forwarded"
  hostname:
    hostname: "http://localhost:8081"
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: "0.5"
      memory: "1250M"
    limits:
      cpu: "6"
      memory: "2250M"
  db:
    vendor: "postgres"
    host: "my-postgresql.postgres.svc.cluster.local"
    port: 5432
    database: "postgres"
    poolMinSize: 2
    poolInitialSize: 2
    poolMaxSize: 2
    usernameSecret:
      name: "my-postgresql"
      key: "postgres-username"
    passwordSecret:
      name: "my-postgresql"
      key: "postgres-password"
  startOptimized: false 
  features:
    enabled:
      - "token-exchange"
      - "admin-fine-grained-authz"
  transaction:
    xaEnabled: false 
  additionalOptions:
    - name: http-max-queued-requests
      value: "1000"
    - name: metrics-enabled 
      value: "true"
    - name: http-pool-max-threads 
      value: "66"
    - name: spi-connections-infinispan-quarkus-site-name
      value: "keycloak"
    - name: hostname-debug
      value: "true"
    - name: http-relative-path
      value: "/"
    - name: http-management-relative-path
      value: "/"


  instances: 1
  ingress:
    enabled: false
  http:
    httpEnabled: true
```
master realm:
- Login with the generated temp-admin
- Navigate to "Realm Settings"
- Change "Realm Name"
- Save


Freshly created realm:
- Create new realm
- Navigate to "Realm Settings"
- Change "Realm Name"
- Save

### Anything else?

It is possible to edit the url in the browser to reference the old realm name and UI will start working again.
